| | | | Friend, Today we’re sharing with you another key pillar of our _**Taking Indiana to the Next Level**_ Plan, which will focus on community development by strengthening our foundation at the local level. **This will be an important step because our local communities are at the heart of what makes Indiana unique, exceptional, and an amazing place to call home. To start, we will:**
* Invest in local community development programs such as the Regional Cities Initiative and the Stellar Communities and Main Street program.
* Enhance the safety of our communities by protecting those who protect us and supporting them with the tools and training necessary to get the job done.
* Address and act on public health, mental health, and drug addiction challenges.
* Protect Indiana’s vulnerable citizens, adults and children alike, from any form of abuse, neglect, or exploitation.

With your help, Indiana will be a state of immense opportunity, a place where every family can grow, every student can achieve, every worker can flourish, every business can thrive, and every citizen can write their own chapter in the story of Hoosier history. **[Join us by pledging your support to strengthen Indiana’s community development.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/b1d027fca5a841579d48f1ade5e0b75b?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=9061a164942f4a8cb6688061edbdf0ec&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_body_cta&utm_term=human-interest&utm_campaign=gfr&utm_content=20161006-what-makes-indiana-unique-16266-petition)**

name: Ben Carson
subject: Who will make the decision in 2016?
body:
Peter,

Walter Judd, a strong anti-communist voice in Congress from 1943
to 1963, once said something that I think is critical for us to
understand as November 8th draws near. He said:

"People often say that, in a democracy, decisions are made by a
majority of the people. Of course, that is not true. Decisions
are made by a majority of those who make themselves heard and who
vote -- a very different thing."

We hear time and time again about America's Judeo-Christian
heritage. But the truth is, these values are nothing but filler
for the history books unless each generation is willing to
breathe new life into them, live by them, and institutionalize
them into our laws and politics.

I accepted the role of Honorary National Chairman of My Faith
Votes because I'm tired of losing.

Just think about the past couple of years. We've seen relentless
attacks on traditional marriage, on the sanctity of life, on the
fundamental right to religious liberty.

Don't think for a moment that there is no correlation between
this, and the fact that tens of millions of registered Christian
voters have been staying home and not going to the polls.

My Faith Votes has been doing incredible work reaching those who
have not been voting, from churches to college campuses, and I
truly believe they will have a big impact on November 8th.

Every single day between now and then matters, and it's time to
pull out all the stops.

I ask you today, right now, to make a tax-deductible donation to
fund this grueling work.

myfaithvotes.z2systems.com/np/clients/myfaithvotes/donation.jsp?campaign=87

Thousands upon thousands of people like you have been extremely
generous, and it's allowed My Faith Votes to really blossom into
an effective organization with an impressive reach. But no month
will be as important as this one. This is the time that their
mettle will be tested.

Thank you for your help today. I wouldn't be asking if it wasn't
important, or if I didn't truly believe it would make a real
difference.

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

name: Marlon Marshall, HillaryClinton.com
subject: Want to help elect Hillary? Commit to Get Out the Vote!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
| Derek --

As the election comes down to the final weeks, I want to tell you about a way you can help Hillary win that’s fun, important, and easy to be a part of -- it’s our Get Out the Vote program.

First of all, let’s get some definitions straight: Get Out the Vote (GOTV for short!) is not a single activity -- it is a coordinated effort that is focused on registering voters, recruiting volunteers, giving voters the information they need to vote, and turning out the vote for Hillary. Who do we need to get involved? **[Everyone!](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjY3MDg5ODUS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTAwOTQwMzQxS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

We’ve done our homework, Derek, and we know that the single most important way to make sure people vote this November is through one-on-one personal interactions. There are plenty of ways to ensure we reach every voter: knocking on doors, attending local events, or making calls -- with GOTV, you’ve got a LOT of choices.

The best part of GOTV is that it’s fun and easy to get involved. You’ll meet plenty of your fellow Hillary supporters, and most importantly, you will directly impact the outcome of this election.

Now more than ever, Hillary needs her core supporters like you to get involved and make a promise to Get Out the Vote. **Pledge to sign up for a shift or two (or more) during the final days before Election Day, and let’s all do our part to ensure Hillary gets into the White House.**
[](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjY3MDg5ODUS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTAwOTQwMzQxS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Get Out the Vote is going to win us this election, Derek -- and I’m excited to see you be a part of it.

Thanks,

Marlon

Marlon Marshall
Director of States and Political Engagement
Hillary for America

|
**Can't donate your time? Chip in now to help support our organizers on the ground:**
| [
](https://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjY3MDg5ODUS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=OTAwOTQwMzQxS0&mt=2&rj=OTAwOTQwMzQxS0&rt=0)

name: Jane Portman
subject: Rob fights for Ohio families
body:
| Peter \--

Rob and I have been married for more than 30 years, and we have three terrific kids.

Rob is working every day to help Ohio families. He’s fighting to give them better opportunities for good paying jobs. He’s promoting policies to ensure a strong national defense in a dangerous world. And his Comprehensive Addiction and Recovery Act (CARA), which was signed into law earlier this year, will help our state begin to turn the tide on the prescription drug and heroin epidemic.

[](https://www.robportman.com/watch_jane?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=jane_ad_blast&n=1)
Despite the gridlock in Washington, Rob is getting things done. More than 45 of his bills have been signed into law, and his work is making a real difference in the lives of people across Ohio. I'm really proud of Rob. I hope you will click [here](https://www.robportman.com/watch_jane?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=jane_ad_blast&n=2) to watch our latest campaign ad to learn more.

Sincerely,

Jane Portman [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=jane_ad_blast&n=3)[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=jane_ad_blast&n=4)

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Leading with our Clean Air Rule
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
This is for our children and grandchildren. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY0MjExMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMDQyNTg3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
| Dear Friend, What would you do to fight for clean air and water for our state? When Washingtonians receive their ballots in a few weeks, their vote will be about a lot more than me versus my opponent. It'll be about leadership. It'll be about our vision for Washington, and what we're willing to do to fight for it. How we combat the climate crisis is one example of what I'm talking about. It's one of the core moral issues of our time. You know that we're already seeing the terrible impacts of climate change: Fires burn, crops dry up, coastal towns flood, and salmon die in hot water. We need to take real action, now. That's why I directed the Department of Ecology to set new standards when the legislature failed to act. I'm proud that this September, the Department of Ecology announced a finalized Clean Air Rule to limit harmful greenhouse gas emissions in Washington. Finalizing the rule is a major step forward in our fight against climate change. [**I hope you 're proud of the progress we're making in Washington. So today, I'm asking you to sign on to a petition to stand with me as we fight to build a clean energy economy that protects our environment and creates well paying jobs for Washingtonians.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY0MjExMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMDQyNTg3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY0MjExMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMDQyNTg3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Republican Bill Bryant says there's nothing we can do to prevent the rise of carbon emissions in Washington. It's a profound abdication of leadership. You and I know differently. You know that we can take action. And you know that we cannot sit by while the terrible effects of climate change ravage our state, our country, and our world. The rule from the Department of Ecology is a big example of what we can do if we work together toward progress. Our agricultural industries, coastal communities, and our beautiful Puget Sound will all benefit. We're setting high environmental standards for the rest of the nation and for countries across the globe. Four years ago, the naysayers said it wasn't possible. Working together for future generations, we've proven the critics wrong. While there's still more to do to protect our environment, the Washington Clean Air Rule provides hope for a cleaner, safer, and sustainable future. [**Now, it 's up to all of us to stand behind this critical progress. Show your support for our newly finalized Clean Air Rule by signing on today!**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY0MjExMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMDQyNTg3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [**SIGN ON »**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=OTY0MjExMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMDQyNTg3OQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

---|---
| **MINTER’S POLLING TELEGRAPHS MORE**
**DESPERATE & DECEITFUL DISTORTION**
Sue Minter’s strategy tells us she is getting increasingly desperate in the final weeks of the campaign.

While Phil has made the economy his priority (see Phil’s plan at [www.PhilScott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=19e133061a&e=19cebfe173)), **Minter has not introduced a comprehensive plan to strengthen the economy or make Vermont affordable.** Instead Minter has indicated she will support new sales taxes, propose a new “cap and trade” fee system on gasoline and diesel, sign a carbon tax on home heating and transportation fuel, and create a “free” education program funded by higher fees on your checking account and ATM transactions. And, unlike Phil Scott, Minter has no plans to modernize state government or address the mismanagement of the last six years. Minter’s vision for Vermont can be summarized in four words: More of the same.

**So, as Election Day draws near, Minter’s plan appears to be to attack Phil with a series of dishonest statements and deceitful distortions.** How do we know? Well, there is her latest ad...But, wait!...there’s more!

Minter’s pollster has also been calling Vermonter’s homes to ask questions about the effectiveness of these misleading attacks. We can only assume that Minter plans to use these dishonest statements in more mail, Internet television and radio advertisements as the election nears.

Vermonters know Phil and they know better than to believe the negative ads. So, we thought we’d save you the time and address the most egregious mistruths here:

| **MINTER’S DECEPTIVE DISTORTIONS** | **PHIL SCOTT’S HONEST FACTS**
---|---
**Phil’s Position & Record on the Gas & Mileage Tax** | This one takes the cake. **Phil has made it clear: He’ll oppose anything that increases transportation costs on families and businesses.** Here’s the truth about the 2009 Transportation Bill (a.k.a. “The T Bill”): Then-Senator Phil Scott stripped out a gas tax proposal proposed and passed by then-Representative Sue Minter and replaced it with a change that was revenue neutral (which means it would not cost drivers more). In the final negotiations between the House and Senate, Sue Minter’s team continued to insist on a higher gas tax, but Phil (even though a member of the minority party) remained staunchly opposed – and his opposition to the gas tax threatened to scuttle months of work on the legislation, which also contained approval for dozens of important local projects. At the end of that process, he reluctantly conceded to a 3-cent gas tax increase, which, he continued to note he was opposed to, in order for the legislation to move forward. And, as a member of the minority, he didn’t have the votes to stop it. As a legislator, you don’t have a veto. As a Governor you do. **Phil will oppose any increase in the cost of transportation on families and businesses and will veto a mileage tax; a carbon tax or a cap-and-trade fee system that adds to the cost of living.**
**Phil’s Position on****Women’s Rights** | Sorry Sue, **Phil is unabashedly pro-choice,** supports a woman’s right to choose and defended Planned Parenthood’s funding when national Republicans tried to defund the organization. Phil also raised two strong, independent daughters and will fight for them and their rights, and the rights of all women.
**Phil’s Record on the Sales Tax** | First, over the period of several months, **Sue has stated on numerous occasions that Vermont should expand its sales tax to services** , citing a commission report that called for adding a tax to 160+ services. This would raise the cost of virtually everything – childcare, haircuts, and other everyday services Vermonters depend on. It would crush seniors on fixed incomes. Now, weeks before the election, she’s running away from her own statements.

Contrary to how Minter falsely distorts Phil’s vote, he voted in 2003 to use one penny on the sales tax to reduce property taxes, which it did. He also voted to reduce the non-residential property tax rate, which is made up, in large part, of Vermont small businesses, not “vacation homeowners” as Minter’s rhetoric implies. The problem was that the Sue Minter’s Democrat majority later refused to follow through on the cost containment commitments made when Act 68 lowered the property tax and Vermonters ended up with a higher sales tax and a higher property tax. This is a point that Governor Jim Douglas has also confirmed. Phil has said this is an important lesson for why we shouldn’t expand the state government’s taxing capacity by adding a sales tax to any service and why **he would veto any expansion of the sales tax to services.**
**Phil’s Support from Employers & Associations**
| Phil’s proud to have the support of thousands of Vermonters. **Nearly 90% of Phil’s total contributions have been from in-state Vermont** workers, families, and small businesses, while **Sue Minter has raised more than 40% of her contributions from out of state** , including thousands from D.C. and New York special interests. Phil will always put what’s best for Vermonters first.
**Phil’s Position on Local Control & Support for Giving Families More School Choice**
| **Phil supports investing in innovation in our classrooms, early education, trades and technical training, and higher education** by reducing overhead costs and finding efficiencies in our education financing system. He supports reforming Act 46 to ensure local control and school choice are preserved. **Sue Minter hasn’t made her position on Act 46 clear** to the voters, but has repeatedly made it clear that she opposes the rights of parents to choose where their child attends school. **Minter has said: “I am not looking to expand [school choice].”**
**Phil’s Position on Education Spending**
| **Phil’s the only candidate to acknowledge that we can provide the best education in the country, while reducing the property tax burden** , if we’re willing to modernize how we’re administering our $1.6 billion education budget for 86,000 children. **Sue Minter doesn’t think Vermont has a property tax problem** and, unlike Phil, she certainly doesn’t have a plan to support innovation in our classrooms.
**Phil’s Plans & Priorities for Combatting the Opiate Epidemic and Ensuring Public Safety** | **Phil has the most comprehensive plan to address the opiate crisis** in this campaign. Phil’s plan addresses prevention, enforcement, and treatment. **Phil has also been endorsed by the Vermont Sheriffs’ Association, the Vermont State Troopers Association and the Vermont Police Association**. Alternatively, Sue Minter has dedicated a mere paragraph on her website to the massive opiate crisis that Vermont is facing and **Minter has received no endorsements from Vermont’s public safety community.** It’s time for Minter to take this issue seriously.
**Phil’s Views on Climate Change & How to Address It**
| Sue, no matter how many times you say it, this statement will never be true. **Phil believes the science:** climate change is real and man made. That’s why **Phil is committed to achieve energy efficiency through a balanced portfolio that includes solar and hydro – all with community input** – rather than through destroying our ridgelines with industrial wind turbines that are forced on communities that do not want them.
**Phil’s Decision to Sell His Business if Elected** | Phil has said he will sell his business if elected. But, let’s break this one down because Minter is working overtime to smear Phil and a long-time family business: First, both the former Secretary of Transportation (Minter’s former boss) and the current Commissioner of Buildings and General Services in the Shumlin Administration have said that no one could influence the bid and contract process. Second, the state process is open, public and fully transparent. Go ahead, make a records request for all the documents, Phil’s opponent already has. And lastly, again, **Phil will sell his company if elected Governor** to focus full time on serving as Governor (that will be a nice change from the incumbent who has been out of state more than 20% of his entire time in office) and avoid any misperceptions while serving. **The very fact that Sue Minter would smear a family business for political gain says all Vermonters need to know about how much she values small businesses in Vermont’s economy.**
**Phil’s Position on Unfunded Business Mandates Like Mandatory Paid Family Leave**
| **Phil has received support from far more Vermont businesses because they understand what’s at stake in this election and want to elect a Governor who will help them create more and better paying jobs.** Meanwhile, Sue Minter continues to take in thousands from out-of-state special interests in New York and D.C. and job-killing environmental groups who want to prevent Vermont’s economy from expanding. **If jobs, the economy and affordability are your top concerns, the choice is crystal clear.**
**But...Selling His Business isn’t Enough!**
| Ah, this issue again. It’s a little sad how desperate they are to distract from the ethical failures of the Shumlin administration. Let’s see if we can help the Minter campaign get it this time: 1) **Phil has said he will sell his company if elected Governor.** 2) The state’s bid process has strict rules, is public, open and transparent and there would be no way for anyone, even a Governor, to influence that process. But that’s beside the point because PHIL IS SELLING HIS BUSINESS. 3) Before Phil announced his decision to sell, the Minter’s own people said he should sell in order to eliminate any perception of a conflict. Seriously. 4) Again, **the very fact that Sue Minter would attack a family business for political gain says all Vermonters need to know about how much she values small businesses in Vermont’s economy.**
**Phil’s Support for****Firearm Safety & Second Amendment Rights** | **Minter is trying to scare voters** – plain and simple. Phil supports the current federal background check law. That’s right, Phil supports the current federal background check law. clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | MINTER’S POLLING TELEGRAPHS MORE DESPERATE & DECEITFUL DISTORTION Sue Minter’s strategy tells us she is getting increasingly desperate in the final weeks of the campaign. While Phil has made the economy his priority (see Phil’s plan at ), Minter has not introduced a comprehensive plan to strengthen the economy or make Vermont affordable. Instead Minter has indicated she will support new sales taxes, propose a new “cap and trade” fee system on gasoline and diesel, sign a carbon tax on home heating and transportation fuel, and create a “free” education program funded by higher fees on your checking account and ATM transactions. And, unlike Phil Scott, Minter has no plans to modernize state government or address the mismanagement of the last six years. Minter’s vision for Vermont can be summarized in four words: More of the same. So, as Election Day draws near, Minter’s plan appears to be to attack Phil with a series of dishonest statements and deceitful distortions. How do we know? Well, there is her latest ad...But, wait!...there’s more! Minter’s pollster has also been calling Vermonter’s homes to ask questions about the effectiveness of these misleading attacks. We can only assume that Minter plans to use these dishonest statements in more mail, Internet television and radio advertisements as the election nears. Vermonters know Phil and they know better than to believe the negative ads. So, we thought we’d save you the time and address the most egregious mistruths here: | MINTER’S DECEPTIVE DISTORTIONS | PHIL SCOTT’S HONEST FACTS ---|--- Phil’s Position & Record on the Gas & Mileage Tax | This one takes the cake. Phil has made it clear: He’ll oppose anything that increases transportation costs on families and businesses. Here’s the truth about the 2009 Transportation Bill (a.k.a. “The T Bill”): Then-Senator Phil Scott stripped out a gas tax proposal proposed and passed by then-Representative Sue Minter and replaced it with a change that was revenue neutral (which means it would not cost drivers more). In the final negotiations between the House and Senate, Sue Minter’s team continued to insist on a higher gas tax, but Phil (even though a member of the minority party) remained staunchly opposed – and his opposition to the gas tax threatened to scuttle months of work on the legislation, which also contained approval for dozens of important local projects. At the end of that process, he reluctantly conceded to a 3-cent gas tax increase, which, he continued to note he was opposed to, in order for the legislation to move forward. And, as a member of the minority, he didn’t have the votes to stop it. As a legislator, you don’t have a veto. As a Governor you do. Phil will oppose any increase in the cost of transportation on families and businesses and will veto a mileage tax; a carbon tax or a cap-and-trade fee system that adds to the cost of living. Phil’s Position onWomen’s Rights | Sorry Sue, Phil is unabashedly pro-choice, supports a woman’s right to choose and defended Planned Parenthood’s funding when national Republicans tried to defund the organization. Phil also raised two strong, independent daughters and will fight for them and their rights, and the rights of all women. Phil’s Record on the Sales Tax | First, over the period of several months, Sue has stated on numerous occasions that Vermont should expand its sales tax to services , citing a commission report that called for adding a tax to 160+ services. This would raise the cost of virtually everything – childcare, haircuts, and other everyday services Vermonters depend on. It would crush seniors on fixed incomes. Now, weeks before the election, she’s running away from her own statements. Contrary to how Minter falsely distorts Phil’s vote, he voted in 2003 to use one penny on the sales tax to reduce property taxes, which it did. He also voted to reduce the non-residential property tax rate, which is made up, in large part, of Vermont small businesses, not “vacation homeowners” as Minter’s rhetoric implies. The problem was that the Sue Minter’s Democrat majority later refused to follow through on the cost containment commitments made when Act 68 lowered the property tax and Vermonters ended up with a higher sales tax and a higher property tax. This is a point that Governor Jim Douglas has also confirmed. Phil has said this is an important lesson for why we shouldn’t expand the state government’s taxing capacity by adding a sales tax to any service and why he would veto any expansion of the sales tax to services. Phil’s Support from Employers & Associations | Phil’s proud to have the support of thousands of Vermonters. Nearly 90% of Phil’s total contributions have been from in-state Vermont workers, families, and small businesses, while Sue Minter has raised more than 40% of her contributions from out of state , including thousands from D.C. and New York special interests. Phil will always put what’s best for Vermonters first. Phil’s Position on Local Control & Support for Giving Families More School Choice | Phil supports investing in innovation in our classrooms, early education, trades and technical training, and higher education by reducing overhead costs and finding efficiencies in our education financing system. He supports reforming Act 46 to ensure local control and school choice are preserved. Sue Minter hasn’t made her position on Act 46 clear to the voters, but has repeatedly made it clear that she opposes the rights of parents to choose where their child attends school. Minter has said: “I am not looking to expand [school choice].” Phil’s Position on Education Spending | Phil’s the only candidate to acknowledge that we can provide the best education in the country, while reducing the property tax burden , if we’re willing to modernize how we’re administering our $1.6 billion education budget for 86,000 children. Sue Minter doesn’t think Vermont has a property tax problem and, unlike Phil, she certainly doesn’t have a plan to support innovation in our classrooms. Phil’s Plans & Priorities for Combatting the Opiate Epidemic and Ensuring Public Safety | Phil has the most comprehensive plan to address the opiate crisis in this campaign. Phil’s plan addresses prevention, enforcement, and treatment. Phil has also been endorsed by the Vermont Sheriffs’ Association, the Vermont State Troopers Association and the Vermont Police Association. Alternatively, Sue Minter has dedicated a mere paragraph on her website to the massive opiate crisis that Vermont is facing and Minter has received no endorsements from Vermont’s public safety community. It’s time for Minter to take this issue seriously. Phil’s Views on Climate Change & How to Address It | Sue, no matter how many times you say it, this statement will never be true. Phil believes the science: climate change is real and man made. That’s why Phil is committed to achieve energy efficiency through a balanced portfolio that includes solar and hydro – all with community input – rather than through destroying our ridgelines with industrial wind turbines that are forced on communities that do not want them. Phil’s Decision to Sell His Business if Elected | Phil has said he will sell his business if elected. But, let’s break this one down because Minter is working overtime to smear Phil and a long-time family business: First, both the former Secretary of Transportation (Minter’s former boss) and the current Commissioner of Buildings and General Services in the Shumlin Administration have said that no one could influence the bid and contract process. Second, the state process is open, public and fully transparent. Go ahead, make a records request for all the documents, Phil’s opponent already has. And lastly, again, Phil will sell his company if elected Governor to focus full time on serving as Governor (that will be a nice change from the incumbent who has been out of state more than 20% of his entire time in office) and avoid any misperceptions while serving. The very fact that Sue Minter would smear a family business for political gain says all Vermonters need to know about how much she values small businesses in Vermont’s economy. Phil’s Position on Unfunded Business Mandates Like Mandatory Paid Family Leave | Phil has received support from far more Vermont businesses because they understand what’s at stake in this election and want to elect a Governor who will help them create more and better paying jobs. Meanwhile, Sue Minter continues to take in thousands from out-of-state special interests in New York and D.C. and job-killing environmental groups who want to prevent Vermont’s economy from expanding. If jobs, the economy and affordability are your top concerns, the choice is crystal clear. But...Selling His Business isn’t Enough! | Ah, this issue again. It’s a little sad how desperate they are to distract from the ethical failures of the Shumlin administration. Let’s see if we can help the Minter campaign get it this time: 1) Phil has said he will sell his company if elected Governor. 2) The state’s bid process has strict rules, is public, open and transparent and there would be no way for anyone, even a Governor, to influence that process. But that’s beside the point because PHIL IS SELLING HIS BUSINESS. 3) Before Phil announced his decision to sell, the Minter’s own people said he should sell in order to eliminate any perception of a conflict. Seriously. 4) Again, the very fact that Sue Minter would attack a family business for political gain says all Vermonters need to know about how much she values small businesses in Vermont’s economy. Phil’s Support forFirearm Safety & Second Amendment Rights | Minter is trying to scare voters – plain and simple. Phil supports the current federal background check law. That’s right, Phil supports the current federal background check law. Phil also recognizes that Vermont is one of the very safest states in the country; and supports enforcing existing gun laws. Phil believes we should focus on the primary causes of violence in Vermont -- mental illness and substance abuse. Trump's team is outraising ours | | Derek, I’ll be honest -- I’m getting really worried that our fundraising is lagging for the third month in a row. We missed our digital fundraising goal in August. Then we missed it by a bigger margin in September. Now we’re just six days into October, and we’re already more than $100,000 off pace.

At the same time, the Trump campaign says they're nearing 3 million individual donors -- while we're stuck at 2.6 million. While our team is falling behind, his supporters are stepping up. That is a really dangerous combination.

Can you believe this week, Friend?

body:
Friend, I first met Chris discussing our different musical tastes, favorite movies and yes -- debating nuclear weapons policy and the nuclear freeze movement. Little did I know that 30 years later his passion to make our communities and our world a safer and better place would still be a defining theme of our lives together.

But I should have guessed, because Chris has always been a strong believer that government can be an important force for good. Sadly, we are living in a time when the radical ideology of groups like the Tea Party are undermining faith in our democracy. For the last 6 years Republican control of the Congress has enabled these extremists to control the agenda and block efforts to make progress.

That's why Washington Republicans and their big-money friends are determined not to lose their majority. They're trying to turn the tide of this election in races across the country--and Maryland won't be an exception.

Over the past few months, top Republicans like Scott Walker and Carly Fiorina have been helping fund our Republican opponent's agenda. They -- just like the NRA and a shady SuperPAC that has already started attacking Chris -- know that our opponent will stand with them if she's elected.

Chris won't.

Chris has spent his time in public service fighting to keep dark money out of politics and end gun violence, and advancing the issues that matter most to the people of Maryland. And when he's elected to the U.S. Senate, his opponent's big-money friends know that he'll keep fighting for our values and priorities.

Early voting begins in Maryland in less than 30 days, friend. Will you chip in $ or whatever you can right now to help keep us reach our September goal before Friday's FEC deadline?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Donate $5 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $10 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $25 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $50 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $100 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $200 to stand with Chris >>>

Or donate another amount to stand with Chris >>>

Polls show that 19% of the electorate in Maryland is still undecided. We need to do better than that. With less than one month until early voting we must do everything we can to turn out as many voters as we possible and move our state and our country forward.

Thank you for being part of this,

Katherine
Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
You're receiving this email at: dpwillis67@gmail.com
If you'd like to unsubscribe visit: http://action.vanhollen.org/unsubscribe
